_meta.source: Curated from CIA World Factbook-style public domain data, reflecting approximately 2022-2024 reporting. Some fields (top exports, top trade partners, labor force composition) shift year-to-year and should be treated as directional.

field_notes.healthcare_system: Broad taxonomy: 'universal_public' (single-payer tax-funded, e.g. UK NHS), 'single_payer' (government-run insurance, e.g. Canada), 'mandatory_multi_payer' (compulsory insurance through multiple funds, e.g. Germany), 'mixed_public_private' (significant parallel systems, e.g. Australia), 'primarily_private' (private market dominant with limited public programs, e.g. USA), 'limited' (low public coverage, predominantly out-of-pocket)
field_notes.languages: Official and/or most widely spoken. Percentages omitted — they vary significantly by census year and methodology.
field_notes.religions: Major traditions only; listed roughly in order of size.
field_notes.top_exports/imports: Categories, not specific products. Reflect recent annual trade data.
field_notes.labor_force_pct: Share of employed labor force in agriculture / industry / services; may not sum to 100 due to rounding or unclassified workers.

USA.spoken_languages_note: Spanish spoken by ~13% of the population; over 350 languages spoken nationally
USA.notable: Third-largest country by area; world's largest economy by nominal GDP.

CAN.spoken_languages_note: Over 20% speak a non-official language at home, including Mandarin, Punjabi, and Spanish
CAN.notable: Second-largest country by area; longest coastline in the world.

MEX.spoken_languages_note: 68 indigenous languages hold co-official status, including Nahuatl and Maya
MEX.notable: Mexico City metropolitan area is one of the largest urban agglomerations in the world.

BRA.spoken_languages_note: Over 180 indigenous languages spoken by small populations
BRA.notable: Fifth-largest country by area; home to ~60% of the Amazon rainforest.

ARG.spoken_languages_note: Italian, German, and Quechua spoken by minorities
ARG.notable: Eighth-largest country by area; one of the world's top beef producers.

CHL.spoken_languages_note: Mapudungun and other indigenous languages recognized regionally
CHL.notable: World's largest copper producer; spans 4,300 km north to south.

COL.spoken_languages_note: 65 indigenous languages officially recognized in their regions
COL.notable: Only South American country with both Caribbean and Pacific coastlines.

PER.notable: Home to Machu Picchu; part of the Andes mountain range spans the country.

GBR.spoken_languages_note: Welsh, Scottish Gaelic, and Irish hold regional official status
GBR.notable: Home of the NHS, founded 1948 — one of the world's first universal healthcare systems.

IRL.spoken_languages_note: English is the primary spoken language; Irish is taught in schools nationally
IRL.notable: Major hub for US tech and pharma multinationals; headline GDP inflated by corporate tax structures.

FRA.spoken_languages_note: Regional languages (Occitan, Breton, Corsican, Alsatian) spoken locally
FRA.notable: Most-visited country in the world; ranked consistently among the top nuclear-energy nations per capita.

DEU.spoken_languages_note: Turkish, Russian, Polish, and English widely spoken in immigrant communities
DEU.notable: World's largest exporter of cars by value; pioneered modern social insurance under Bismarck (1880s).

ITA.spoken_languages_note: German, French, Slovenian hold regional official status; many local dialects
ITA.notable: Most UNESCO World Heritage sites of any country; no statutory minimum wage — wages set by sector collective bargaining.

ESP.spoken_languages_note: Catalan, Galician, and Basque hold regional co-official status
ESP.notable: Second-most-visited country in the world; among the highest life expectancies globally.

PRT.spoken_languages_note: Mirandese recognized as co-official in one region
PRT.notable: Oldest continuously existing nation-state in Europe; its language is official in nine countries.

NLD.capital: Amsterdam (The Hague is seat of government)
NLD.spoken_languages_note: Frisian co-official in Friesland; English near-universal as second language
NLD.notable: About 26% of land is below sea level; Port of Rotterdam is Europe's largest seaport.

BEL.spoken_languages_note: Language boundaries map roughly to regions: Flanders (Dutch), Wallonia (French), East Cantons (German)
BEL.notable: Hosts EU institutions and NATO headquarters; once held the world record for going without a government (589 days).

CHE.notable: Direct-democracy model with frequent referendums; no statutory minimum wage — left to cantonal or sectoral rules.

AUT.spoken_languages_note: Slovene, Croatian, and Hungarian recognized as regional official languages
AUT.notable: Vienna routinely ranks among the world's most livable cities; no statutory minimum wage.

SWE.spoken_languages_note: Five recognized minority languages: Finnish, Sami, Meänkieli, Romani, Yiddish
SWE.notable: No statutory minimum wage — wages set by collective bargaining covering ~90% of workers.

NOR.spoken_languages_note: Two written Norwegian standards coexist; Sami holds regional co-official status in the north
NOR.notable: Largest sovereign wealth fund in the world (~$1.5 trillion), funded by oil revenue.

DNK.spoken_languages_note: Faroese and Greenlandic official in their respective autonomous regions
DNK.notable: No statutory minimum wage; 'flexicurity' model pairs easy hiring/firing with strong safety net.

FIN.spoken_languages_note: Swedish spoken natively by ~5% of the population; Sami holds regional status
FIN.notable: Ranked #1 in the World Happiness Report for seven consecutive years (2018–2024).

POL.spoken_languages_note: Several minority languages recognized regionally (Silesian, Kashubian, German)
POL.notable: Largest economy in Central and Eastern Europe; one of the EU's fastest-growing economies.

GRC.spoken_languages_note: Small communities speak Turkish, Albanian, and Macedonian Slavic
GRC.notable: Over 6,000 islands and islets; tourism alone accounts for ~20% of GDP.

TUR.spoken_languages_note: Kurdish spoken by ~15% of the population (mostly in the southeast)
TUR.notable: Only country spanning two continents (Europe and Asia); Istanbul is Europe's largest city by population.

RUS.spoken_languages_note: 35+ co-official languages at the republic level (Tatar, Chechen, Bashkir, etc.)
RUS.notable: Largest country by area — spans 11 time zones.

UKR.spoken_languages_note: Russian widely spoken, especially in eastern and southern regions
UKR.notable: Largest country entirely within Europe; major global grain exporter.

ISR.capital: Jerusalem (claimed; most embassies in Tel Aviv)
ISR.spoken_languages_note: English widely spoken; Russian common among immigrant communities
ISR.notable: Highest share of GDP spent on civilian R&D in the world.

SAU.spoken_languages_note: English widely used in business and government
SAU.notable: World's largest oil exporter; holder of the two holiest cities in Islam (Mecca and Medina).

ARE.government: Federal absolute monarchy (federation of seven emirates)
ARE.spoken_languages_note: English is the common language of business; majority of residents are non-citizen expatriates
ARE.notable: About 88% of residents are foreign-born — one of the highest shares in the world.

IRN.spoken_languages_note: Azeri, Kurdish, Arabic, and Baluchi spoken by large regional minorities
IRN.notable: Fourth-largest oil reserves and second-largest natural gas reserves in the world.

EGY.spoken_languages_note: English and French widely used in business and education
EGY.notable: Suez Canal carries ~12% of global trade; ~95% of population lives along the Nile corridor.

MAR.spoken_languages_note: French widely used in business, government, and higher education
MAR.notable: Holds ~70% of the world's phosphate reserves; straddles a UNESCO-recognized biodiversity hotspot.

NGA.spoken_languages_note: 500+ indigenous languages; Hausa, Yoruba, and Igbo are most widely spoken
NGA.notable: Most populous country in Africa; Lagos is one of the fastest-growing megacities in the world.

ZAF.spoken_languages_note: Zulu and Xhosa are most widely spoken as first languages; English dominates business
ZAF.notable: One of the world's most unequal societies by Gini coefficient; three capital cities for different branches of government.

KEN.spoken_languages_note: 60+ indigenous languages; Kikuyu, Luhya, and Kalenjin among the largest
KEN.notable: Global leader in mobile money adoption — M-Pesa processes transactions equivalent to ~50% of GDP.

GHA.spoken_languages_note: Akan, Ewe, Ga, and Dagbani among the most widely spoken indigenous languages
GHA.notable: World's second-largest cocoa producer; one of the more stable democracies in West Africa.

ETH.spoken_languages_note: 80+ indigenous languages; Oromo is the most widely spoken as a first language
ETH.notable: Only African country never formally colonized; uses a unique 13-month calendar.

CHN.government: One-party socialist republic (Communist Party single-party state)
CHN.spoken_languages_note: Cantonese, Shanghainese, Hokkien, and dozens of other regional varieties; 55 recognized ethnic minority languages
CHN.notable: World's most populous country (recently surpassed by India); world's largest manufacturer by output.

JPN.spoken_languages_note: Ryukyuan languages spoken in Okinawa; Ainu in parts of Hokkaido
JPN.notable: One of the world's oldest populations — ~29% over age 65; highest life expectancy globally.

KOR.spoken_languages_note: English widely taught as a second language
KOR.notable: Lowest fertility rate in the world (~0.72 in 2023); global cultural export powerhouse (K-pop, K-drama, gaming).

IND.spoken_languages_note: Bengali, Marathi, Telugu, Tamil, Gujarati, and Urdu among the largest; 121+ languages with 10,000+ speakers
IND.notable: World's most populous country since 2023; largest film industry by output (Bollywood and regional).

PAK.spoken_languages_note: Punjabi, Pashto, Sindhi, Saraiki, and Balochi widely spoken as first languages
PAK.notable: Fifth-most-populous country; home to K2 and other peaks exceeding 8,000 meters.

BGD.spoken_languages_note: English widely used in business, higher education, and government
BGD.notable: One of the most densely populated countries in the world; world's second-largest garment exporter after China.

IDN.spoken_languages_note: 700+ indigenous languages; Javanese, Sundanese, and Madurese among the most widely spoken
IDN.notable: World's largest archipelagic state — over 17,500 islands; fourth-most-populous country.

VNM.spoken_languages_note: English, French, and Chinese used in business; 50+ recognized minority languages
VNM.notable: One of the fastest-growing economies in Asia over the past two decades; major electronics manufacturing hub.

THA.spoken_languages_note: Regional variants (Isan, Northern Thai, Southern Thai) widely spoken
THA.notable: Only Southeast Asian country never colonized by a European power; Bangkok is the most-visited city in the world by international arrivals.

PHL.spoken_languages_note: 180+ indigenous languages; Cebuano, Ilocano, and Hiligaynon among the largest

MYS.spoken_languages_note: English, Mandarin, Tamil, and regional Chinese dialects widely spoken
MYS.notable: Rotating monarchy — kingship passes among nine hereditary state rulers every five years.

SGP.spoken_languages_note: English is the primary language of business and instruction; 'Singlish' is widely spoken informally
SGP.notable: One of the most densely populated countries; world's busiest transshipment port.

AUS.spoken_languages_note: Mandarin, Arabic, Vietnamese, and Italian are the largest languages other than English; 150+ Indigenous languages still spoken
AUS.notable: Sixth-largest country by area with one of the lowest population densities; Medicare provides universal coverage alongside a private system.

NZL.spoken_languages_note: English is dominant; Māori used in official and ceremonial contexts
NZL.notable: First country to give women the right to vote (1893); sheep outnumber humans roughly five to one.
